chapter_title.ar: باب شَهَادَةِ الشَّرِيكِ وَ الْأَجِيرِ وَ الْوَصِيِّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – The testimony of the partner, and the employee, and the trustee

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/245)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/245)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رِفْقَةٍ كَانُوا فِي طَرِيقٍ فَقُطِعَ عَلَيْهِمُ الطَّرِيقُ فَأَخَذُوا اللُّصُوصَ فَشَهِدَ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ قَالَ لَا تُقْبَلُ شَهَادَتُهُمْ إِلَّا بِإِقْرَارٍ مِنَ اللُّصُوصِ أَوْ شَهَادَةٍ مِنْ غَيْرِهِمْ عَلَيْهِمْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Ali Bin Asbaat, from Muhammad Bin Al Salt who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> about friends who were on a road, and it was cut off to them (by bandits). So the thieves were seized, so they testified against each other. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Their testimonies are not acceptable except by the confession from the thieves, or a witness from others against them’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Ribat from Muhammad ibn al-Salt who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of certain friends who were on a journey when their road was blocked, and they caught the thieves, then certain ones of them testified against the others. He (the Imam) said, ‘Their testimony is not admissible unless the thieves confess or others testify against them.’”
